description_long: This is a traditional windmill with a cylindrical stone tower base and a conical, reddish-brown tiled roof. Four large wooden blades are attached to a central hub on the front, extending outward in a cross pattern. The structure features small arched windows with wooden frames and a single arched wooden door at the base, suggesting an entry point. The lower portion of the tower is clad in blue-gray stonework, while the upper section is finished in a lighter plaster. The windmill's blades are constructed from wood and canvas, with visible support beams and spars. The overall design is rustic and functional, evoking historical European rural architecture.
description: A rustic cylindrical windmill with a stone base, conical tiled roof, and four large wooden blades, featuring arched windows and a wooden door.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main door, two windows, and the full cross of the windmill blades.
description_view_1: Side-rear view highlighting the window detail, the angled roof, and the side profile of the blades.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying two arched windows and the conical roof, with the blades seen edge-on.
description_view_3: Angled rear view showing the entryway, two windows, and the side of the windmill blades.
